title: Arianne wants to work on Eva again
display_title: Arianne wants to work on Eva again
wikitext:
[]: Arianne wants to work on Eva again             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Wed Jul 03, 2019 5:10 pm Not sure if this is the right place for it, but Arianne (original singer of Komm, süsser Tod and Everything You've Ever Dreamed) has just tweeted she is interesting in working on Evangelion again. It might be too late, though.. https://twitter.com/iconsmashmybox/stat ... 30277?s=20 https://www.reddit.com/r/evangelion/com ... urce=share

[]: Re: Arianne wants to work on Eva again             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Jul 04, 2019 8:38 am Would be so epic if she was in the last rebuild tho

[]: Re: Arianne wants to work on Eva again             Quote Post by robersora » Thu Jul 04, 2019 7:56 pm I wish her the best of luck.

[]: Re: Arianne wants to work on Eva again             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Fri Jul 05, 2019 4:46 pm That'd be something! I've always wanted her to come back in some form.

[]: Re: Arianne wants to work on Eva again             Quote Post by MistaL » Wed Aug 14, 2019 6:21 pm Oh hey, I can give some input into this! I started talking with Arianne a while ago to ask if she had an isolated vocal track for her Komm, Susser Tod cover she did in 2012. She didn't, but offered to record a new one for me, and I thanked her and mentioned it would be cool if she could work with Sagisu again someday, since he keeps using the same recording of the song for his various different mixes even though the original recording doesn't always fit very well. She was curious about it, and then I also mentioned that Eva is still an ongoing property with the movies, which I guess she actually didn't know, and she and I thought it'd be a cool idea to see if she could have a song in 3.0+1.0. She was excited about the idea so I gave her Sagisu's and Khara's Twitter, as well as Kanemitsu's since he works for Khara and is more likely to respond. Kanemitsu DM'd her and seemed to be mistaken about what she was talking about, and somehow thought she was referring to rerecording the song for the Netflix version for some reason. She clarified to him that she was referring to the upcoming final film, but he never got back to her after that. If anyone has any other idea for how she could contact Khara or Sagisu about collaborating in the future, it would be greatly appreciated.
